commonIssues:
Missing grandparent or parent caste proof, non-availability of family tree certificates (Vamshavruksha), and income/creamy layer certificate disputes.

How to fix:

  • Apply for a Vamshavruksha (Family Tree) certificate from the local Village Accountant (VA) or Taluk Office.
  • Obtain old land records (RTC/Phani) from the Bhoomi Land Records Portal — these often mention caste.
  • Submit school admission registers of parents/grandparents from the respective schools.
  • Get a sworn affidavit from two community elders verified by a notary.

Name, date of birth, or address in the application does not match the Aadhaar card, leading to immediate rejection.

How to fix:

  • Update your Aadhaar details at UIDAI My Aadhaar Portal before applying for the caste certificate.
  • Ensure the name, DOB, and address exactly match across Aadhaar, school records, and application form.
  • If the name on Aadhaar uses Kannada transliteration, ensure the English spelling matches other documents.
  • Visit the nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra for corrections if online update is not possible.

Non-receipt of OTP, file upload failures (wrong format/size), and 'server busy' errors on the Nadakacheri or Seva Sindhu portals.

How to fix:

  • Access Nadakacheri Portal during off-peak hours (early morning).
  • Upload documents in JPEG/PDF under 500KB — compress using SmallPDF.
  • If OTP is not received, check if your mobile number is registered correctly and try after 5 minutes.
  • Use Google Chrome (latest version), clear cache and cookies before retrying.
  • For persistent issues, apply via Seva Sindhu or visit the nearest Karnataka One Centre.

Confusion regarding sub-category selection for Scheduled Castes (SC) — Category A, B, or C — as per current rules.

How to fix:

  • Check your exact sub-category from the official Karnataka Government notification — available at the Social Welfare Department website.
  • Consult the Taluk Social Welfare Office for clarification on your specific caste sub-group.
  • Refer to the Karnataka Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes (Reservation of Posts) Act for the official caste list.
  • If incorrect sub-category was selected, apply for a correction at the Tahsildar office with supporting documents.

Mistakes in entering caste, sub-caste, or income details lead to rejection or incorrect certificates.

How to fix:

  • Double-check all entered details against your original documents before submission.
  • Verify the exact caste and sub-caste spelling from your father's or family's existing certificate.
  • If already submitted with errors, apply for certificate correction via Nadakacheri Portal.
  • Visit the Tahsildar office with the incorrect certificate and supporting documents for rectification.

Applications get stuck at the Village Accountant (VA) level for extended periods, causing applicants to miss educational or employment deadlines.

How to fix:

  • Track status on Nadakacheri Portal using your application number.
  • If delayed beyond 15 working days, escalate to the Tahsildar in writing.
  • File a grievance on the Karnataka Janaspandana Grievance Portal.
  • Invoke the Karnataka Sakala (Guarantee of Services to Citizens) Act — services must be delivered within the guaranteed timeline.

Rejection during physical verification by officials due to inability to prove community status.

How to fix:

  • Provide multiple supporting documents: old RTC records, school records of parents, community certificates of relatives.
  • Request the Village Accountant to verify your family's records in the village register.
  • If rejected, file an appeal with the Assistant Commissioner (AC) of the sub-division.
  • Seek legal aid from the District Legal Services Authority at NALSA for free assistance.

Inability to download the certificate from the Nadakacheri portal due to non-uploading of the document or errors in certificate printing (misspelled names, wrong category).

How to fix:

  • Visit the Tahsildar office and request them to upload the approved certificate to the Nadakacheri Portal.
  • If certificate has printing errors, apply for correction at the Tahsildar office with supporting proof.
  • Try downloading using a different browser (Chrome recommended) and check if pop-up blocker is disabled.
  • Contact the Karnataka Helpline 080-22230282 or file a grievance at Janaspandana.
